en.extract:
The Arabian wheatear (Oenanthe lugentoides) is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is found in Oman, Saudi Arabia and Yemen.


Subspecies
There are two recognised subspecies:

Oenanthe lugentoides lugentoides, the nominate subspecies.
Oenanthe lugentoides boscaweni, Oman


References

The '''Arabian wheatear''' ('''''Oenanthe lugentoides''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Muscicapidae]]. It is found in [[Oman]], [[Saudi Arabia]] and [[Yemen]].

== Subspecies ==
There are two recognised subspecies:<ref>{{cite web |title=Chats, Old World flycatchers|url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/chats/ |editor=Gill F, D Donsker |editor2= P Rasmussen |name-list-style=amp |year=2023 |website=IOC World Bird List |version=13.2|doi=10.14344/IOC.ML.10.2}}</ref>
*''Oenanthe lugentoides lugentoides'', the [[nominate subspecies]].
*''Oenanthe lugentoides boscaweni'', Oman<ref name=IUCN/>
